response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated ability to work with distributed, high-throughput systems at Sociomantic. However, the role's core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust for systems-level routing pipeline work — is critically unmet. The candidate is primarily a TypeScript/Node.js engineer; their C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis, and C++ does not even appear in their current skills list. There is no Rust experience at all. The geospatial affinity is a genuine signal but insufficient to offset the systems programming gap for a mid-senior C++ IC role.
strengths: Geospatial and GIS domain familiarity via GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform used in production) and Green Map web GIS application
Distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic, building low-latency microservices processing millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Broad Linux and DevOps background including CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, and server-side systems across multiple roles
concerns: No viable C++ or Rust proficiency — C++ is absent from the skills section, and the only C++ experience is a ~6-month role from 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis
No experience with routing tile generation, navigation guidance pipelines, or geospatial data transformation at the map-data layer
Career trajectory is firmly TypeScript/Node.js/web; current and recent roles show no movement toward systems programming
